verdict_reasoning: HSPD1 (HSP60) is canonically a mitochondrial matrix chaperonin, but it is well-documented to appear on the plasma membrane outer surface under stress, apoptosis, tumor transformation, and inflammatory conditions. Cell-surface HSP60 has been detected on tumor cells and stressed cells via flow cytometry and is recognized by antibodies, NK cells, and experimental immunotherapeutics. It lacks a classical TM domain or GPI anchor but associates with the outer leaflet in a cell-state-dependent manner. Because surface exposure requires specific conditions (stress/disease state), verdict is contextual.
